drafts.draft: Most small businesses don't have a marketing problem. They have a follow-up problem. The leads are already there. Nobody chases them on Tuesday at 6pm.
If a customer asks at 9pm and you answer at 9am, the sale is already gone. Speed has quietly become the only differentiator left.
Founders spend a year building the perfect onboarding then lose 40% of signups because nobody replies to 'is this legit?' for six hours.

drafts.target_text:
Add Codex seats with a $0 seat fee for a limited time.

Through the end of June, eligible ChatGPT Business and Enterprise customers can add Codex-only seats, making it easier to give more developers a
drafts.draft: The interesting unlock isn't writing code faster. It's that the cost of throwing away the first attempt has dropped to almost zero.

drafts.target_text:
2010:
“Let’s hire a huge engineering team.”

2026:
“Wait… Claude can build most of this.”

2030:
“Yeah we probably need humans again.”
drafts.draft: The pendulum on this swings hard both ways every six months. The teams shipping the most output have stopped paying attention to either side of the argument.

drafts.target_text: User research and testing should never be an afterthought.
drafts.draft: The thing nobody can fake: software that respects your time. Most 'feel' debates are downstream of that one decision.

drafts.target_text:
Completely agree with this

Money won’t make you happy

Especially if you trade all your time for it

But it for sure makes your life way better and less stressful when used well
drafts.draft: The framing that finally clicked for me: time and money trade against each other until you build something that runs without you. That's the actual asset.

drafts.target_text:
CATL just signed a three-year, 60 gigawatt-hour sodium-ion battery deal - the largest commercial sodium-ion agreement in history.

Sodium is cheaper, more abundant, and less geographically concentrate
drafts.draft: The part of these announcements that ages best: not the headline number, but who's now locked into a multi-year roadmap they can't easily change.
